term:culture: Will you be considering dynamic melting pot cultures?
I fear for the uniqueness of each culture and government otherwise.
term:system: I'd be genuinely shocked if bloodlines weren't in - it's a big part of the focus on dynasties and characters, and is something that will go hand-in-hand with the new cadet branch system.
Along with a revamped papacy system I don't understand why these aren't lock-ins for release.
term:character: Game mechanics concerns aside - as you'll build them over the shoulders of a giant I am confident you'll deliver - now see if you can still take care of the childish, cartoonish characters populating the few screenshots released.
I'd be genuinely shocked if bloodlines weren't in - it's a big part of the focus on dynasties and characters, and is something that will go hand-in-hand with the new cadet branch system.
term:court: Over the top court physicians come to mind, whereas medieval medicine was pretty interesting and not always as primitive as leeches.
Balancing the power between the Great Names of my court, and bringing in new blood through bastards or recently un-seated nobles from nearby kingdoms made the game a lot of fun.
term:characters: Game mechanics concerns aside - as you'll build them over the shoulders of a giant I am confident you'll deliver - now see if you can still take care of the childish, cartoonish characters populating the few screenshots released.
I'd be genuinely shocked if bloodlines weren't in - it's a big part of the focus on dynasties and characters, and is something that will go hand-in-hand with the new cadet branch system.
term:dlc: We'll be disappointed for as long as we remember the lack of nomads or merchant republics; no doubt the moment we forget, they'll be included in a new DLC.
Lock them behind a Supernatural DLC later on.
edge:cavalry+infantry: Able to set up units in formation (Example Calvary or Light Infantry(You might not be cavalry Culture) in the Flanks .
Tactically speaking, the pike square hadn't been invented yet, and cavalry often used lances longer than the pikes of the infantry.
edge:silk+silver: I am not talking about what happens after Silk & Silver.
We are not changing the holding system for Silk & Silver, but whenever we say "city", we really do mean castles/cities/temples, as all of them have cities in them, effectively.
edge:iberian+peninsula: However in the case of the Iberian Peninsula, which is a major battleground between faiths, borders would be decided over the lines of these major rivers (mainly Douro and Tagus).
But, ahem, since we are discussing issues in representing the Iberian Peninsula, are we, like, ever to have a discussion about patronyms before CK3 arrives?
edge:commander+flank: So armies don't have 3 commanders 1 for each flank anymore?
Although I also enjoyed being able to play around with my flank composition and commanders.
edge:feast+hunt: I consider hunting more similar to feasts from CK2 - you prepare them once in a while, there's an opportunity to gather your lords and neighbours, make friends and enemies but that's all.
I think it will be more along the lines of "Take the Hunting decision X times" to gain Hunter, same as "Take the Feast decision X times" for Carouser, and "Duel X people" for Duelist.
edge:archer+cavalry: (lack of horses to equip their own large scale horse archer cavalry to counter the nomads, like the Tang successfully did earlier, also made Song vulnerable to Mongol invasions in 13th century) Great dev diary!
Steppe cultures will for example, have access to Horse Archers, while Greeks have access to superior Heavy Cavalry in the form of Cataphracts.
phrase:royal court: As soon as we can, we will share the date of the next batch of fixes & improvements with you I want new pc dlc dd Would've been better received if we'd actually get a patch fixing the bugs with royal court first.
This has nothing to with Royal Court and is made by a separate dev team.
phrase:roman empire: I like playing the country that destroyed the Roman Empire.
I'll be blunt: As long as the french monarchy, the holy roman empire, the polish crown and the byzantine empire play different, since their feudalisms all were vastly different, Im happy.
phrase:middle ages: Contrary to what many people think there was very little witch hunting in the middle ages.
This is only changed in the late middle ages and the bulk of the witch hunts happened centuries later.
phrase:silk road: Will trade routes be integrated into the Silk Road mechanic from All Under Heaven in some capacity?
It would be weird to have trade in game but still have the Silk Road mechanic be only about innovations and not, well, trade.
phrase:holy roman empire: I'll be blunt: As long as the french monarchy, the holy roman empire, the polish crown and the byzantine empire play different, since their feudalisms all were vastly different, Im happy.
That's HEAVY when you see the Holy Roman Empire's history, or the Byzantium one, or basically every big arabian dynasty.
phrase:wards and wardens: Also I vote for Wards and Wardens Objectively correct choice Does it mean we are going to build roads in CK3?
When it comes to the event packs, I think all three of these options are interesting, but I voted for Wards and Wardens.
